subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: The topic is communicated accurately with clear explanation of CSR focus, firm background, and key terms.
The research question is explicitly stated, well-focused on LâOrÃ©al and its CSR impact, and revisited in the conclusion.
An appropriate, coherent methodology is applied, including a four-pillar framework and relevant secondary sources.
weaknesses: Exposition occasionally drifts into descriptive CSR narrative without tightly linking back to the research question.
Combining two outcome variables (competitive advantage and financial performance) broadens the scope and prevents fully comprehensive coverage.
The range of sources is adequate but leans heavily on corporate reports, lacking triangulation or primary research.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place the work solidly in the mid-band (3â4) for all three strands. The studentâs communication and explanation are clear and generally effective, the research question is focused though somewhat broad, and the methodology is appropriate but not sufficiently robust for the top band. A mark of 4 reflects good performance with room for deeper focus and methodological breadth.

strengths: Source material is applied in a mostly relevant and appropriate way, with well-referenced operational data and metrics.
Subject knowledge of CSR concepts and business frameworks is clear and coherent throughout the essay.
Terminology (e.g., CSR, Porterâs differentiation, ESG scores) is generally accurate and consistently used.
weaknesses: Heavy reliance on LâOrÃ©alâs own publications introduces bias and weakens critical validity.
Some claims are asserted without external corroboration, and a few numerical data points lack inline citations.
Occasional lapses in precision (confusing similar profit metrics) and descriptive tangents reduce overall sophistication.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates a clear and coherent grasp of concepts with mostly appropriate application of sources, placing it in the 3â4 band. However, reliance on corporate publications, occasional inaccuracies, and lack of deeper literature integration prevent a higher mark. A score of 3 accurately reflects these strengths and limitations.

strengths: Research quality is strong, with a good breadth of financial, ESG, and strategic data clearly relevant to the question.
Analytical frameworks (ratio analysis, SWOT, Porterâs strategies) are applied to generate insights into CSRâs impact.
A coherent line of argument is developed, culminating in a concise conclusion linking CSR initiatives to performance outcomes.
weaknesses: Analysis is often descriptive, reporting improvements without rigorously interrogating causality or alternative explanations.
Critical evaluation is limited: counter-arguments and triangulation with independent academic sources are sparse.
Discussion acknowledges limitations (e.g., data bias, pandemic impacts) only briefly, lacking the depth for top-band evaluation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments show research quality in the 7â9 band, with analysis depth and evaluation in the 4â6 band. The overall Critical Thinking performance thus resides at the upper end of the mid-range. A mark of 9 reflects good, relevant research and clear analysis, tempered by descriptive tendencies and limited critical depth.

strengths: The essay structure includes all required elements (title page, abstract/introduction, table of contents, headings, conclusion, bibliography).
Layout conventions (readable font, labelled tables/figures, in-text citations) are applied consistently.
The table of contents and section headings aid navigation and overall coherence.
weaknesses: Pagination is inconsistent and some tables and paragraphs are misaligned, disrupting flow.
Dense blocks of text and occasional drafting errors (incomplete sentences) hinder readability and polish.
Presentation lacks the meticulous formatting and visual clarity needed for the highest band.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structural and formatting elements are all present, and the layout is functional and generally clear, placing the work in the mid-band for Presentation. Minor lapses in pagination, paragraphing, and polish prevent full marks. A score of 3 reflects solid performance with room for refinement.

strandSelections.reasoning:
â¢ The research questionââTo what extent has LâOrÃ©alâs CSR strategy contributed to its competitive advantage and financial performance in the global beauty industry?ââis explicitly stated and revisited in the conclusion.  
â¢ It is sufficiently focused on a single company and two specific outcome variables, but combining both competitive advantage and financial performance broadens the scope and makes complete coverage difficult within 4,000 words.  
â¢ The connection between the question and subsequent discussion is evident, yet at times the analysis diverts into detailed CSR descriptions that do not always advance the central enquiry, indicating partial rather than full focus.

â¢ The discussion draws on recognised frameworks to construct a line of argument that CSR contributes to both competitive differentiation and financial gains, culminating in a concise conclusion.  
â¢ Evaluation is present but mainly reiterative; limitations (e.g., over-reliance on company data, pandemic impacts) are acknowledged only briefly, and alternative viewpoints or counter-arguments are thin.  
â¢ Consequently, the argument is âbasicâ and partially critical, satisfying the 4â6 band but lacking the depth, balance and insight necessary for higher bands.

â¢ The essay demonstrates clear understanding of CSR, sustainability, SWOT, Porterâs strategies and ratio analysis, applying them to LâOrÃ©alâs context with reasonable accuracy.  
â¢ Concepts are explained but often remain at a textbook level; discussion of how CSR drives competitive advantage lacks depth on industry dynamics or stakeholder theory, showing partial rather than full mastery.  
â¢ Use of sources supports the knowledge displayed, yet reliance on descriptive passages and limited critical literature means the studentâs command, while coherent, is not consistently sophisticated.

â¢ Business-management terminologyâCSR, ESG, gross profit margin, Porterâs differentiationâis generally accurate and incorporated throughout the essay.  
â¢ There are occasional lapses (e.g., confusing ânet profit marginâ with âprofit before interest and taxâ) and some stylistic repetition, indicating that understanding, while sound, is not flawless.  
â¢ Overall, terms and theories are used mostly correctly but without the consistent precision and sophistication characteristic of the top band.

â¢ The student conducts ratio trends, SWOT and Porter analyses to probe links between CSR and performance; these provide some clear insights (e.g., link between SPOT tool and cost leadership).  
â¢ However, much of the analysis is descriptiveâreporting improvements without interrogating causality or alternative explanations. Assertions that CSR âled toâ higher margins or share price outperformance are not rigorously evidenced.  
â¢ The reasoning is thus clear but only moderately supported, aligning with the mid-range of the 4â6 band rather than demonstrating the depth and sophistication required for higher marks.

â¢ Most data, quotations and financial figures used are relevant to the CSR focus and are referenced, showing largely appropriate application.  
â¢ Nonetheless, heavy dependence on LâOrÃ©alâs own publications risks bias, and some claims (e.g., causal links between CSR launches and margin changes) are asserted without external corroboration.  
â¢ Isolated instances of tangential material (detailed pillar descriptions without analytical follow-up) reduce overall appropriateness, placing performance solidly in the middle band.

â¢ The investigation relies mainly on company annual reports, CSR reports and businessânews websites, supplemented by a course textbook; these sources are generally relevant but weighted heavily toward corporate self-reporting.  
â¢ The student shows some conscious selection of data tables (e.g., operating profit, COâ reductions) and applies standard BM tools (SWOT, ratios, Porter).  
â¢ However, the range is modestâthere is limited use of independent academic studies or industry databasesâand there is little evidence of primary research or triangulation, so the methodology is judged appropriate but not robust enough for the highest band.

â¢ The student assembles a good quantity of dataâfinancial statements (2012-2023), ratio calculations, ESG scores and CSR outputsâthat are clearly relevant to the research question.  
â¢ Evidence is largely secondary and sometimes uncritical of corporate self-reporting, yet it does provide a coherent base for analysis of both competitive advantage and financial results.  
â¢ The breadth across financial, strategic and sustainability sources meets the descriptor for âgoodâ research, though limited triangulation and scant primary or academic data prevent a move into the excellent (10â12) band.

â¢ The student applies standard EE formatting conventions: readable font, section headings, in-text citations with superscripts and a compiled bibliography.  
â¢ Most layout requirements (title page, word count, numbered pages, tables/figures labelled) are present, though some page numbers are omitted and table formatting is occasionally misaligned, affecting professional presentation.  
â¢ Overall, layout is acceptable and functional, aligning with the 3â4 band, yet lacks the meticulous accuracy and polish needed for full marks in Presentation.

â¢ The student states the key concept of sustainability, names the firm, and clearly outlines the CSR focus in both the title and the opening paragraph.  
â¢ Background information on LâOrÃ©alâs history, mission and successive CSR programmes is adequate and allows the reader to understand why the issue is significant. Key terms such as âcompetitive advantageâ and âfinancial performanceâ are explained, though occasionally in a narrative rather than fully analytical manner.  
â¢ While generally clear and complete, the exposition sometimes drifts into lengthy description of CSR initiatives without linking each element back to the research focus; this prevents the communication from reaching the comprehensive standard required for the top band.

â¢ The essay contains all required structural elements: title page with word count, abstract/ introduction, table of contents, main body with clear section headings, conclusion and bibliography.  
â¢ The sequence is logical and generally supports reader navigation; however, pagination on several pages is missing/misaligned and some sub-sections (e.g., split tables) interrupt flow, showing minor lapses in appropriateness.  
â¢ Given these small inconsistencies, the work meets the descriptors for an appropriate structure but does not achieve the seamless coherence characteristic of the top level.

annotations.comment: The opening uses a rhetorical question rather than a concise statement; a more direct introduction would strengthen clarity and alignment with the research question.
Analysis remains largely descriptive, narrating CSR activities without critically linking each initiative back to competitive advantage or financial performance.
Footnotes are used to reference primary sources, demonstrating appropriate application of source material in the early discussion.
Heavy reliance on LâOrÃ©alâs own publications risks bias; integrate independent academic or industry studies to triangulate findings and strengthen validity.
Chronological summary of CSR commitments provides a coherent narrative, helping connect historical context to the research focus.
Repeated identical footnotes and inconsistent formatting distract the reader; streamline citations and maintain a consistent style throughout.
The four-pillar framework is clearly introduced, presenting a structured methodology for evaluating CSR initiatives.
Lacks critical evaluation of how each pillar directly affects competitive advantage or profit metrics; link descriptive pillars to financial outcomes.
Long paragraphs hinder readability; consider using subheadings or bullet points for each pillar to improve presentation and reader engagement.
The title page lacks a visible page number; ensure consistent pagination to satisfy IB formatting standards.
The table of contents lists all main sections and corresponding pages clearly, aiding reader navigation through the extended essay.
Describes achievements without analyzing their strategic significance; expand on how these results enhance competitive differentiation.
Footnote numbers restart inconsistently, disrupting reference flow; use continuous numbering and consistent styling in the bibliography.
Integrates detailed quantitative metrics (e.g., renewable raw material percentages) to support CSR impact analysis, demonstrating solid data application.
Lacks citation for the RSPO certification data and zero-deforestation achievements; provide specific source references to verify these claims.
This dense block of text would benefit from subheadings or a small table to improve clarity and visual presentation of key figures.

The title clearly states the investigation focus and includes both competitive advantage and financial performance, situating the research question precisely within the global beauty industry context.
The research question combines two outcome variables, which broadens scope and makes full coverage difficult; consider narrowing to one primary outcome to improve focus.
The word count is provided on the title page, meeting IB formatting requirements and giving transparency to the report length.
Demonstrates subject knowledge by describing consumer feedback mechanisms under the âLiving sustainabilityâ pillar.
Provides impressive figures but omits analysis of causality; link these five key figures back explicitly to financial or competitive outcomes.
Clearly outlines the two key CSR programmes by name and date, setting up a structured evaluation of their impact on the research question.
Connects SPOT tool methodology to European Product Environmental Footprint standards, reflecting effective integration of external benchmarks.
The table of contents is split awkwardly across pages, disrupting flow; consider fitting it on a single page for seamless presentation.
Provides clear background on LâOrÃ©alâs mission and values, demonstrating appropriate use of source material to build foundational subject knowledge.
The sentence ending âSince the 1980s, asâ is incomplete, indicating a drafting error; ensure all sentences are fully rendered to maintain professional presentation.
Employs precise operational metrics (energy consumption reduction, ISO 50001 certification rates) to illustrate CSR cost-saving effects.
No inline citation for waste-management statistics; insert source at point of data entry to enhance credibility of numerical evidence.
Paragraph exceeds page width and breaks awkwardly; split into two or add sub-sections to maintain reading flow.
The â5Dâ campaign description lacks a clear citation format for the methodology; ensure source formatting aligns with IB guidance.
Demonstrates understanding of social impact metrics, citing number of employees with disabilities and social program reach.
Lacks critical evaluation of how social engagement actions translate into competitive advantage; discuss impacts on brand equity or loyalty.
Footnote is cluttered with long URLs; consider using shortened or parenthetical references to improve readability.
Visual presentation of key figures (icons with percentages) effectively summarises CSR achievements for quick comprehension.
